bathymetry.B02_Blue_458_523nm: PRIMARY depth penetration (~15-25m in clear water)
bathymetry.B04_Red_650_680nm: Surface reference (separate water column from bottom)
bathymetry.B08_NIR_785_900nm: Water surface/atmospheric correction - does NOT penetrate water

methods.stumpf_ratio: Depth = m1 * ln(n * R_blue) / ln(n * R_green) + m0
methods.lyzenga: Depth = a * ln(R_blue) + b * ln(R_green) + c

signatures.exposed_steel_hull: High VV, low VH (smooth metal = corner reflector)
signatures.submerged_wreck: Moderate VV, high VH (volume scattering through water)
signatures.oil_hydrocarbon_sheen: Very low VV, very low VH (smooth surface dampens capillary waves)
signatures.mussel_covered_wreck: Moderate VV, moderate VH (rough biological surface)

orbit_passes.ascending: ~06:00 local, 6-12 day revisit, morning surface, lower wind
orbit_passes.descending: ~18:00 local, 6-12 day revisit, BETTER for Great Lakes (afternoon wind creates capillary waves, submerged steel creates smooth shadow)

B11_SWIR.purpose: Oil/fuel sheen detection. Hydrocarbons absorb SWIR; slick appears as DARK anomaly relative to open water.
B11_SWIR.note: On HLS L30, B11 = Landsat B6 SWIR. On HLS S30, B11 = Sentinel-2 B11 SWIR.

B04_Red.purpose: Thin oil sheens show slightly elevated red reflectance. Cross-reference with B11 dark.

SAR_smooth_dark.purpose: Hydrocarbon slicks suppress capillary waves. Appear as smooth-DARK patch: BOTH VV and VH anomalously low.
SAR_smooth_dark.distinction: Submerged wreck = bright (corner reflector). Oil slick = dark (damped surface). These are opposite signatures.

wake_suppression.description: Ship wakes are linear, narrow (<3 pixels wide), and fade within 2km. A genuine oil slick or hydrocarbon anomaly is diffuse (aspect_ratio < 8). Reject detections where bounding box length/width > 8.

source_classification.wreck_seep: Persistent (multi-date) diffuse slick directly over a submerged anomaly (thermal + SAR lock nearby)
source_classification.pipeline_leak: Linear/elongated slick aligned with known infrastructure corridor (Line 5 runs ~NNE-SSW through Straits)
source_classification.surface_vessel: Narrow linear feature, width 1-2 pixels, fades within 5km — apply wake_suppression
source_classification.unknown_diffuse: Diffuse, single-date, no co-located thermal/SAR anomaly — log and flag for re-observation

line5_corridor.description: Enbridge Line 5 dual pipeline crosses Straits of Mackinac on the lake bed
line5_corridor.note: Any SWIR-dark anomaly within 0.01 degrees of this corridor should be tagged line5_candidate
